hypothesis: If task-tool alignment is high—i.e., tools' capabilities closely match task requirements—then scientific LLM agents will achieve substantially higher success rates and scores (e.g., pass@5) across environments regardless of LLM (Claude-3.5 Sonnet vs GPT-4o) or agent scaffold (ReAct vs tool-calling), and this improvement will exceed performance gains from changing framework choice or increasing tool description verbosity.
